message:
chore(beads): close r73v + 1rzz, file 3 r73v follow-ups

* r73v closed: state-equivalent cross-impl loadrdb gate shipped in
  9c460ed; byte-identical / AOF / LZF deferred to ri1u, 1uin, 91kt.
* 1rzz closed: DB recovery procedure shipped via hs8w sister bead
  (br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check OK, counts.db_vs_jsonl
  OK 806/806, sync.metadata in sync).
* Filed: ri1u (AOF cross-impl), 1uin (LZF compression),
  91kt (byte-identical re-emit / compact-type encoder).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-1pe7","title":"[HIGH] DEBUG DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE/OBJECT parity — 48 divergences across digest-computation + DEBUG OBJECT encoding/addr/serialize fields","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_debug_matches_runtime (48 cases after enable-debug-command yes): (a) DEBUG DIGEST / DIGEST-VALUE — fr-runtime doesn't implement the xor-SHA1-of-per-key-digest algorithm upstream uses, so every digest case diverges. (b) DEBUG OBJECT — upstream reply is 'Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<enc> serializedlength:N lru:... lru_seconds_idle:N type:<type>'; ours emits a shorter 'refcount:1 encoding:<enc>' without the pointer/lru/serialized fields. (c) DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE invalid-value / wrong-arity wording differs. (d) Case-insensitive DEBUG subcommand dispatch — upstream normalises, ours may not. Fix: wire DEBUG DIGEST / DIGEST-VALUE in fr-runtime + fill in the full DEBUG OBJECT reply + align SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE error wording + lowercase dispatch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T13:25:45.276247292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T23:13:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T23:13:00Z","close_reason":"Already resolved: live_redis_core_debug_matches_runtime passes 100% under STRICT mode. DEBUG OBJECT + DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE canonicalizers + SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE permissive parse + upstream-wording fixes all landed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-1qdg","title":"Implement real DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, RELOAD, and OBJECT subcommands","description":"# Implement real DEBUG subcommands

## What
Three DEBUG subcommands currently return OK without doing anything:
1. DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0/1: should toggle the active-expire cycle on/off
2. DEBUG RELOAD: should save + reload the dataset (equivalent to SAVE + restart load)
3. DEBUG OBJECT <key>: should return encoding, refcount, serializedlength, lru info

## Implementation

### DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
- Add pub active_expire_enabled: bool to Store (default true)
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 sets it to false, 1 sets it to true
- The runtime's active_expire_cycle checks this flag before running
- dispatch_argv needs &mut Store access (already has it)

### DEBUG RELOAD
- This is a runtime-level operation (needs to call persist_snapshot_to_disk then load_aof)
- In fr-command, we can't do this. Two options:
  a) Add a flag to Store (debug_reload_requested) that the runtime checks after dispatch
  b) Accept that DEBUG RELOAD only works from the runtime path (not from Lua)
- Recommendation: option (a) — add the flag, runtime checks it after each command batch

### DEBUG OBJECT <key>
- dispatch_argv has access to Store, which has object_encoding()
- Return format: \"Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<encoding> serializedlength:<len> lru:<idle> lru_seconds_idle:<idle_s>\"
- encoding: from store.object_encoding()
- serializedlength: from store.memory_usage_for_key()
- lru/idle: from store.object_idletime()
- refcount: always 1 (same as Redis for non-shared objects)

### DEBUG JMAP
- Keep as no-op — JVM thread dump is N/A for Rust. Return OK.

## Testing
- Unit test: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0, verify active_expire_enabled is false
- Unit test: DEBUG OBJECT <key> returns correct encoding string
- Conformance: add DEBUG OBJECT cases

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (debug_cmd)
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (active_expire_enabled field)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check active_expire_enabled, check debug_reload_requested)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:07.010370056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267730522Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267460231Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated DB from JSONL, verify doctor/status/show, then promote without deleting old DB artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-25T01:47:41.473719041Z","created_by":"codex-beads-repair","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:25:03.643357040Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:25:03.640812365Z","close_reason":"DB recovery shipped via hs8w sister bead. Procedure documented + executed: (1) cp .beads/beads.db backup; (2) br doctor --repair (rebuilds blocked_issues_cache); (3) when 'br sync --import-only' fails with UNIQUE constraint due to duplicate-row corruption, mv the canonical .beads/beads.db{,-wal} aside; (4) br init --prefix frankenredis; (5) br sync --import-only --rebuild (imports JSONL→fresh DB cleanly). Current state verified: 'br doctor' reports sqlite.integrity_check OK, counts.db_vs_jsonl OK (806/806), sync.metadata in sync, schema.tables OK, schema.columns OK, write_probe OK. Snapshots preserved at .beads/beads.db.backup_orphan_recovery_1777347889 and .beads/beads.db.corrupt_orphan_recovery_1777347912 (gitignored). The 'snapshot, rebuild isolated, verify, promote' acceptance criterion has been met end-to-end.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-1uin","title":"[HIGH] r73v sub-task (c): LZF compression on RDB save","description":"Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r73v: implement upstream-compatible LZF compression on the fr-persist RDB write side. Today fr-persist::rdb_encode_string emits raw length-prefixed strings; upstream prefers LZF (encoding byte 0xC3 with the 0x40 special-encoding flag) for strings >= 16 bytes (configurable via rdbcompression). fr-persist already DEcompresses LZF on read (see lzf_decompress in fr-persist/src/lib.rs:1212). Implement the matching LZF compressor (~80 lines, mirrors upstream's lzf_c.c::lzf_compress). Wire into rdb_encode_string with a heuristic threshold + 'compressed-fits' check. Acceptance: encode/decode round-trip is identity AND fr-persist's emitted dump.rdb loads cleanly under upstream redis-server when long strings are present.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:43:46.321875790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:43:46.321875790Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-1v8b","title":"Implement real REPLICAOF TCP client that connects to primary and completes PSYNC handshake","description":"# REPLICAOF TCP Client

## What
When `REPLICAOF host port` is executed (or --replicaof CLI flag), FrankenRedis should actually connect to the specified primary via TCP, complete the PSYNC handshake, and enter replication mode.

## Current State
The existing `drive_replica_sync()` in fr-server/src/main.rs has the connection scaffolding including PING, AUTH, REPLCONF, and PSYNC handshake steps. The `ReplicaPrimaryConnection` struct exists. The `replication_follow_up_bytes()` function exists. The issue is that REPLICAOF command in fr-runtime returns OK but does not trigger the actual connection — it only sets config state.

## Implementation
1. In fr-runtime, REPLICAOF should set `server.replicaof_host` and `server.replicaof_port` fields (add if missing)
2. In fr-server main loop, check if replicaof is configured but no ReplicaPrimaryConnection exists → initiate connection
3. The existing `drive_replica_sync()` already handles the handshake FSM — verify it works with a real Redis primary
4. After FULLRESYNC: receive RDB snapshot bytes, decode with fr_persist::decode_rdb, apply via apply_rdb_entries_to_store
5. After CONTINUE: receive backlog bytes, parse as RESP commands, execute through runtime
6. Handle connection failure: set retry timer, log error, try again after backoff

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: REPLICAOF host port sets config fields correctly
- Test 2: REPLICAOF NO ONE clears config and stops replication
- Test 3: INFO replication shows role:slave when replicaof is set
- Test 4: INFO replication shows master_link_status:down before connection succeeds

## Integration Tests (in fr-server tests)
- Test 5: Start legacy redis-server, start frankenredis with --replicaof, verify PING/REPLCONF/PSYNC handshake completes (check INFO for master_link_status:up)
- Test 6: After handshake, SET on primary → GET on replica returns the value
- Test 7: Connection drops → replica detects and schedules retry

## Logging
- Log each handshake step with timestamp: "REPL: sending PING to primary", "REPL: received PONG", etc.
- Log FULLRESYNC/CONTINUE decision and offset
- Log snapshot size and apply time
- Log connection failures with retry schedule

## Files
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (REPLICAOF command handler, config fields)
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (connection initiation, snapshot apply)
- crates/fr-server/tests/replication_e2e.rs (integration tests)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.889343993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T05:22:51.278623941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T05:22:51.278359060Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-1zxx","title":"Add benchmark regression gate to CI/scripts pipeline","description":"# Benchmark Regression Gate

## What
A script that runs the benchmark suite, compares against checked-in baselines, and fails if p99 degrades by more than 10% or throughput drops by more than 15%.

## Why
Performance regressions are silent killers. The Performance Doctrine in AGENTS.md requires "gate persistence overhead and replication-lag regressions" but no gate exists.

## Implementation
1. scripts/benchmark_gate.sh:
   - Start frankenredis in background
   - Run fr_benchmark with standard workloads
   - Compare against baselines/*.json
   - Report deltas
   - Exit non-zero if regression detected
2. Configurable thresholds via env vars
3. Output: delta report artifact in artifacts/benchmark/

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (fr_benchmark binary)
- Initial baselines recorded","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:23.224067917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:56:05.922840524Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:56:05.922656586Z","close_reason":"Implemented benchmark regression gate script, docs, and guarded workflow integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1zxx","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-gess","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.702954088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-8xmq","title":"Downgrade FEATURE_PARITY.md replication status to honest assessment","description":"# Honest Replication Status

## What
Change FEATURE_PARITY.md "Replication baseline | parity_green" to "parity_gap" or "in_progress" until cross-process replication actually works.

## Why
The current "parity_green" rating for replication is dishonest. The state machine logic works in isolation but no bytes flow between processes. Honesty about project status is a prerequisite for good engineering.

## Implementation
1. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md: change replication row status to "in_progress"
2. Update notes to honestly describe what works (FSM logic, PSYNC decision math, offset tracking) and what does not (actual TCP replication)
3. After replication epic is complete, upgrade back to parity_green with evidence

## No Dependencies
This should be done immediately, before any replication implementation work.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:17.272701785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:52:37.119459043Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:52:37.119284593Z","close_reason":"Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md replication row from parity_green to in_progress with honest assessment of what works (TCP replication, PSYNC, FULLRESYNC) and what does not (runtime REPLICAOF command, replica-of-replica chains, FR-to-FR E2E, diskless replication)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-90iz","title":"Align XREAD BLOCK timeout validation with Redis integer-ms semantics","description":"Redis requires XREAD/XREADGROUP BLOCK to be an integer millisecond timeout and rejects out-of-range values. FrankenRedis currently validates BLOCK as a float in fr-command and uses saturating_add in fr-server, which can accept fractional or oversized inputs incorrectly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:04:58.923207545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:17:17.667520443Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:17:17.667268236Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-91kt","title":"[HIGH] r73v sub-task (a-ext): byte-identical re-emit (compact-type encoder selection)","description":"Stretch acceptance for br-frankenredis-r73v: 're-emit byte-identical on save'. The 9c460ed live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream gate proves STATE-equivalence cross-impl, but the bytes differ because fr-persist::encode_rdb always emits canonical type tags (RDB_TYPE_LIST=1, RDB_TYPE_SET=2, RDB_TYPE_HASH=4, RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2=5) where upstream picks the COMPACT form (LIST_QUICKLIST_2=18, SET_INTSET=11, SET_LISTPACK=20, HASH_LISTPACK=16, ZSET_LISTPACK=17) for shapes that fit its size thresholds. Wire fr-persist::encode_rdb to mirror upstream's compact-type selection (re-using the encoder helpers that already exist in fr-store::dump_key for DUMP/RESTORE). Acceptance: encode_rdb(decode_rdb(upstream_dump)) == upstream_dump for all shapes within compact thresholds.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:43:46.515668824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:43:46.515668824Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-icsm","title":"EPIC: Establish real performance baseline with redis-benchmark-compatible harness and p50/p95/p99 latency tracking","description":"# Performance Evidence Epic

## Why This Is Critical
The README describes "Deterministic Latency Replication Core (DLRC)" as the "Crown-Jewel Innovation" and claims "profile-proven performance" with "p95/p99 latency tracking." Yet the repo contains ZERO throughput benchmarks, ZERO latency percentile measurements, and an empty baselines/ directory. The only checked-in artifacts measure conformance test execution speed (~1.3ms), not Redis workload performance.

This is the single most dangerous gap because performance is the stated reason this project exists as a differentiator from legacy Redis. Without evidence, DLRC is marketing copy, not engineering.

## What Success Looks Like
- A redis-benchmark-compatible harness that measures ops/sec, latency histograms (p50/p95/p99/p999), and memory usage under mixed workloads
- Baseline numbers checked into baselines/ with comparison against legacy Redis on identical workloads
- CI-gatable regression detection: any commit that degrades p99 by >10% fails the gate
- The README performance claims are either proven or honestly revised

## Architecture
The benchmark harness should be a new binary in fr-server or a dedicated crate that:
1. Connects via real TCP to a running frankenredis instance
2. Runs configurable workloads (SET/GET mixed, pipeline depth, key sizes)
3. Records latency histograms using HdrHistogram or equivalent
4. Outputs JSON artifacts compatible with the existing artifacts/ structure
5. Compares against baselines/ and reports deltas

## Dependencies
All sub-tasks feed into this epic.  {"id":"frankenredis-r71v","title":"[HIGH] Reality-check vs upstream Redis 7+: CLUSTER FORGET/COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, ACL GETUSER selectors, XADD MINID/LIMIT, XSETID ENTRIESADDED","description":"Run a focused parity audit against vendored redis-server (7.2.4) for the Redis 7-era surface that hasn't been live-oracle exercised yet. Specifically:

(a) CLUSTER subcommands beyond MYID/SHARDS/SLOTS/INFO: FORGET, COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, RESET, BUMPEPOCH, FLUSHSLOTS, ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE, LINKS. Verify error wording when cluster mode is disabled and arity errors against upstream's exact phrasing.
(b) ACL GETUSER selectors field (Redis 7.0+ multi-selector support): the field is structurally present in our reply but the channel/key/command vectors per selector may not match upstream when the user has zero selectors vs N>0 selectors.
(c) XADD MINID and LIMIT options (Redis 7.0+): we accept them but their effect on stream trimming + the +OK/(nil)/error reply shape needs upstream verification.
(d) XSETID with ENTRIESADDED and MAXDELETEDID arguments (Redis 7.2+).

 {"id":"frankenredis-r72v","title":"[HIGH] RESP3 protocol parity: HELLO 3 push-frame routing + map/set/double/big-number types in real handlers","description":"Our parser learned RESP3 reply types via ParserConfig::allow_resp3 (br-frankenredis-ozcx), but the runtime handlers haven't been audited end-to-end for the RESP3-only reply shapes:

(a) HELLO 3: server response should include a Map instead of an Array for the metadata; verify client receives '%' framing not '*'.
(b) Push frames ('>' prefix): pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, and tracking redirects should switch to push frames when the session's resp_protocol_version == 3. Today we still emit Arrays even after HELLO 3.
(c) Map ('%') replies for HGETALL/CONFIG GET/XINFO STREAM in RESP3 — upstream switches to Map; verify we mirror that.
(d) Set ('~') replies for SMEMBERS/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF in RESP3.
(e) Double (',') replies for ZSCORE/ZADD INCR in RESP3.
(f) Big number ('(') support for very large counters.
(g) Verbatim string ('=') for INFO/CLUSTER NODES.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 and re-run the relevant cases, comparing against upstream's RESP3 wire frames.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:50:23.174897054Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T03:50:23.173045710Z","close_reason":"Completed in fc3d541","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hello","protocol","push","resp3"]}
-{"id":"frankenredis-r73v","title":"[HIGH] RDB+AOF persistence round-trip parity: load upstream-produced .rdb + .aof and re-emit byte-identical on save","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip) to RDB + AOF persistence:

(a) Load a corpus of upstream-produced dump.rdb files covering every type tag we emit (string, list_quicklist_2, set/intset/listpack, hash/listpack, zset_2/listpack, stream_listpacks_3, plus 7.4 hash_metadata family). Verify each loads cleanly and matches the in-memory state upstream would produce. Resave; feed back to upstream redis-server --loadrdb; verify XINFO STREAM FULL / HGETALL / etc. parity.
(b) Same for AOF: load upstream-produced appendonly.aof files (with RDB-preamble + multi-DB SELECT + EXPIREAT + complex transactions), verify replay + resave path is byte-stable.
(c) Re-encode our RDB with --rdbcompression yes (upstream's default) and verify upstream loads the LZF-encoded strings. Today we never emit LZF on save.
(d) Verify the RDB version header (REDIS0011 for 7.2, REDIS0012 for 7.4) matches what we negotiate.

 {"id":"frankenredis-r74v","title":"[HIGH] Pub/Sub scalability + keyspace notifications: SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH (sharded), CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS, K/E notify masks","description":"(a) Sharded pub/sub (Redis 7+): SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH against shard-channels. CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS / SHARDNUMSUB introspection. Our implementation routes shard pub/sub through the same global hash-table as PUBLISH — needs a separate shard map keyed by hash-slot owner.
(b) Keyspace notifications: CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events 'KEA' should turn on __keyspace@<db>__:<key> and __keyevent@<db>__:<event> message generation for every applicable command. Today our notify mask is parsed but only a subset of commands actually fire events. Wire the full Redis event taxonomy (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/expire/evicted/new/loaded/trim).
(c) NUMSUB regression: NUMSUB on a single channel with N subscribers should return [channel, N] not [channel, 0] when subscriptions are split across multiple processes (matters once we add cluster mode).
(d) PUBSUB NUMPAT counts unique pattern subscriptions across all clients.

 {"id":"frankenredis-r75v","title":"[HIGH] Lua scripting parity audit: EVAL/EVALSHA/EVAL_RO + FUNCTION CREATE/LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE + script-side redis.* surface","description":"Reality-check our Lua execution path against vendored redis-server:

(a) EVAL with multiple keys + args, error propagation, return value coercion (status reply, integer, bulk string, nested array, redis-error vs runtime-error).
(b) EVALSHA cache hit/miss, SCRIPT EXISTS, SCRIPT LOAD, SCRIPT FLUSH ASYNC/SYNC.
(c) EVAL_RO + EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement: any write call inside RO must return -READONLY.
(d) FUNCTION LOAD with the !lua engine + meta header, FUNCTION CALL, FUNCTION FLUSH, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+).
(e) The redis.* surface inside scripts: redis.call, redis.pcall, redis.error_reply, redis.status_reply, redis.sha1hex, redis.log, redis.replicate_commands(), redis.breakpoint(), redis.debug(), redis.set_repl(), KEYS / ARGV indexing semantics.
(f) MULTI inside EVAL: should be rejected; EXEC inside EVAL should error.
(g) FCALL_RO + script-flag #!lua flags=no-writes / allow-stale / allow-oom / allow-cross-slot-keys.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_scripting.json + core_function.json with each combination, run live-oracle diff.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:30:00Z","close_reason":"Major slices shipped: (c) EVAL_RO/EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement covered by existing tests. (d) FUNCTION LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE binary format tracked under r83v.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["eval","function","lua","scripting"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r80v","title":"[HIGH] RESP3 push frame routing: pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, tracking redirects must use '>' framing on RESP3 sessions","description":"After HELLO 3, upstream Redis switches asynchronous-server-message paths from RESP2 Array to RESP3 Push frame ('>' prefix per https://github.com/redis/redis-specifications/blob/master/protocol/RESP3.md#push-type). Today our runtime continues to emit Arrays:

(a) PUBSUB messages: when a SUBSCRIBE'd client receives a PUBLISH, the ["message", channel, payload] reply must be a Push, not an Array. Same for ["smessage", ...] (sharded), ["pmessage", pattern, channel, payload], ["subscribe"|"unsubscribe"|"psubscribe"|"punsubscribe"|"ssubscribe"|"sunsubscribe", channel, count].

(b) Client-side caching invalidations: with CLIENT TRACKING ON in RESP3 mode, the server pushes ["invalidate", [keys...]] frames to the tracking redirect target on key mutation. Today our runtime queues these on the wrong stream and as Array.

(c) Server-side notifications (when more land): keyspace notifications via CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events route through the same pub/sub layer; verify the push framing follows.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend the live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 on the dedicated connection used for these cases, then assert the wire frame on the receiving side starts with the '>' byte under RESP3 (and '*' under RESP2).

Scope guard: this bead only covers the framing of asynchronous-server-message paths. Synchronous request/reply RESP3 promotions (HGETALL→Map, SMEMBERS→Set, ZSCORE→Double) are tracked separately under br-frankenredis-r72v.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:15:56.813244354Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T05:15:56.811371439Z","close_reason":"Completed in 5c83cec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["client-tracking","pubsub","push","resp3"]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-rk0","title":"Align replication offsets with byte-stream semantics","description":"Replication currently advances primary/ack offsets by one per AOF record, while PSYNC backlog replay and replica propagation operate on RESP byte streams.
